task: Draft a Twitter/X launch tweet for askr that positions the product authentically without revealing it's unreleased, and identify high-signal accounts to follow for organic reach.
discussion_summary: User has tweeted about problems faced while building with Claude but hasn't announced solutions yet. askr launches in ~1 week. User rejected generic solution-announcement tweets as weak and inauthentic. Final direction: lead with sarcasm about Claude's friction points (context loss across sessions, handoff overhead), position askr as the fix without naming it yet, and follow accounts (@alexalbert__, @simonw, @swyx, @karpathy, etc.) who already have the audience and discuss Claude workflows.

accomplishments.what: Identified three core pain points askr solves: session context loss, machine switching friction, and handoff/explanation overhead
Generated list of 10 high-signal accounts to follow for organic reach and authentic engagement
Rejected weak positioning ('been building the fix') in favor of sarcasm-led tweet that hints at solution without revealing product

in_progress.what: Final copy-pastable tweet text with sarcasm opener about Claude friction, waiting for user to confirm tone before posting

next_actions.action: Provide final copy-pastable tweet text (sarcasm opener + 3 pain points) that user can paste directly into X without editing
next_actions.why: User explicitly requested copy-pastable format; session ended mid-delivery of final tweet

next_actions.action: Follow the 10 identified accounts (@alexalbert__, @simonw, @swyx, @karpathy, @amasad, @emollick, @garrytan, @levelsio, @marc_louvion, @kunal0dha) and engage authentically on their Claude-related posts
next_actions.why: Builds credibility and reach before public launch; these accounts already discuss the problem space askr solves

next_actions.action: Schedule tweet for 1 week before public launch (6 days from now) to keep arc alive without premature reveal
next_actions.why: Maintains momentum from previous problem-focused tweets while building anticipation for solution reveal

next_actions.action: Prepare GitHub launch thread and README GIFs (from Phase 4 roadmap) for public launch in 7 days
next_actions.why: Tweet is teaser; actual launch requires polished materials and install story

decisions.decision: Use sarcasm/irony tone ('Damn building with claude makes my life so much easier') rather than earnest problem statement
decisions.reason: User rejected earnest framing as 'gay as hell'; sarcasm lands better with technical audience and positions user as someone who understands the pain deeply

decisions.decision: Mention three pain points (context loss, machine switching, handoff overhead) rather than two
decisions.reason: Two problems make askr look narrow; three establish a pattern and justify a dedicated tool

decisions.decision: Do not mention askr by name or reveal it's a product in this tweet
decisions.reason: Product launches in 1 week; tweet is teaser to keep narrative arc alive, not announcement

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Tweet: 'been building the fix for both. one week out.'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: makes askr look weak, doesn't capture all the problems askr solves
